perturbation.original_text: Within [***] days after the conclusion of each Calendar Quarter to occur during the Term, commencing with the First Commercial Sale of the Product in the Field in the Territory, MMT shall deliver to SIGA a written report containing the following information (each a "Quarterly Report"): [***]. All such reports shall be considered Confidential Information of MMT. Concurrent with the delivery of the applicable Quarterly Report, MMT shall pay to SIGA in Dollars by wire transfer of immediately available funds into an account designated by SIGA in writing in advance of such payment the net result of Quarterly Collected Revenues, minus the corresponding Promotion Fee, and minus any applicable Credit Amount for such Calendar Quarter (all amounts as calculated in the Quarterly Report, and the payment of the net result being a "Quarterly Payment").
perturbation.changed_text: Within [***] days after the conclusion of each Calendar Quarter to occur during the Term, commencing with the First Commercial Sale of the Product in the Field in the Territory, MMT shall deliver to SIGA a written report containing the following information (each a "Quarterly Report"): [***]. All such reports shall be considered Confidential Information of MMT. Concurrent with the delivery of the applicable Quarterly Report, MMT shall pay to SIGA in Dollars by wire transfer of immediately available funds into an account designated by SIGA in writing in advance of such payment the net result of Quarterly Collected Revenues, minus the corresponding Promotion Fee, and minus any applicable Credit Amount for such Calendar Quarter (all amounts as calculated in the Quarterly Report, and the payment of the net result being a "Quarterly Payment"). **MMT has the option to delay the Quarterly Payment for up to 60 days after the delivery of the Quarterly Report without incurring any penalties or interest.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the payment deadline. The original text implies that the payment should be made concurrently with the delivery of the quarterly report. By adding the sentence about MMT having the option to delay the payment for up to 60 days, it contradicts the original intention of immediate payment, creating uncertainty and conflicting terms. This new text in Section 6.2(a) contradicts the immediate payment requirement, causing in-text contradiction about when the payment is actually due.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Within [***] days after the conclusion of each Calendar Quarter to occur during the Term, commencing with the First Commercial Sale of the Product in the Field in the Territory, MMT shall deliver to SIGA a written report containing the following information (each a "Quarterly Report"): [***]. All such reports shall be considered Confidential Information of MMT. Concurrent with the delivery of the applicable Quarterly Report, MMT shall pay to SIGA in Dollars by wire transfer of immediately available funds into an account designated by SIGA in writing in advance of such payment the net result of Quarterly Collected Revenues, minus the corresponding Promotion Fee, and minus any applicable Credit Amount for such Calendar Quarter (all amounts as calculated in the Quarterly Report, and the payment of the net result being a "Quarterly Payment"). **MMT has the option to delay the Quarterly Payment for up to 60 days after the delivery of the Quarterly Report without incurring any penalties or interest.**
perturbation.justification: The original text states that payment is due "concurrent with the delivery of the applicable Quarterly Report." The changed text adds a clause allowing MMT to delay the payment for up to 60 days after the report. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the payment timeline. The contradicted text which is essentially the same as the original now conflicts with the new added sentence.

perturbation.original_text: The Indemnified Party may participate in and monitor such defense with counsel of its own choosing at its sole expense; provided, however, the Indemnifying Party shall assume and conduct the defense of the Claim and may so defend any such Claim with counsel of its choosing. The Indemnifying Party will not settle any Claim without the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party, not to be unreasonably withheld, unless the settlement involves only the payment of money by the Indemnifying Party. So long as the Indemnifying Party is actively defending the Claim in good faith, the Indemnified Party will not settle or compromise any such Claim without the prior written consent of the Indemnifying Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Indemnified Party may participate in and monitor such defense with counsel of its own choosing at its sole expense; provided, however, the Indemnifying Party shall assume and conduct the defense of the Claim and may so defend any such Claim with counsel of its choosing. **Notwithstanding the above, the Indemnified Party reserves the right to independently settle the claim at any point, regardless of the Indemnifying Party's involvement.** The Indemnifying Party will not settle any Claim without the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party, not to be unreasonably withheld, unless the settlement involves only the payment of money by the Indemnifying Party. So long as the Indemnifying Party is actively defending the Claim in good faith, the Indemnified Party will not settle or compromise any such Claim without the prior written consent of the Indemnifying Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change inserts an in-text contradiction in Section 9.3 by giving the Indemnified Party the right to settle a claim independently at any point. This directly conflicts with the subsequent clauses that require the Indemnified Party to obtain consent from the Indemnifying Party for settlements, particularly while the Indemnifying Party is actively defending the claim in good faith. The added sentence creates an uncertainty regarding the control over settlement decisions and creates contradictory rules about consent. In-text Contradiction in Section 9.3, about Indemnification Procedures, between who actually can settle the claims when having an Indemnifying Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Party claiming indemnity under this ARTICLE 9 (the "Indemnified Party") shall give written notice to the Party from whom indemnity is being sought (the "Indemnifying Party") promptly after becoming aware of a Claim for which indemnity may be sought hereunder (it being understood and agreed, however, that the failure by an Indemnified Party to give notice of a Claim for which indemnity may be sought as provided in this Section 9.3 shall not relieve the Indemnifying Party of its indemnification obligation under this Agreement except that in no event shall the Indemnifying Party be liable for any Losses that result from any delay in providing such notice). The Indemnified Party will provide the Indemnifying Party with reasonable assistance, at the Indemnifying Party's reasonable expense, in connection with the defense of the Claim for which indemnity is being sought. The Indemnified Party may participate in and monitor such defense with counsel of its own choosing at its sole expense; provided, however, the Indemnifying Party shall assume and conduct the defense of the Claim and may so defend any such Claim with counsel of its choosing. **Notwithstanding the above, the Indemnified Party reserves the right to independently settle the claim at any point, regardless of the Indemnifying Party's involvement.** The Indemnifying Party will not settle any Claim without the prior written consent of the Indemnified Party, not to be unreasonably withheld, unless the settlement involves only the payment of money by the Indemnifying Party. So long as the Indemnifying Party is actively defending the Claim in good faith, the Indemnified Party will not settle or compromise any such Claim without the prior written consent of the Indemnifying Party. If the Indemnifying Party does not assume and conduct the defense of the Claim as provided above, (a) the Indemnified Party may defend against, consent to the entry of any judgment, or enter into any settlement with respect to such Claim in any manner the Indemnified Party may deem reasonably appropriate (and the Indemnified Party need not consult with, or obtain any consent from, the Indemnifying Party in connection therewith), and (b) the Indemnifying Party will remain responsible to indemnify the Indemnified Party as provided in this ARTICLE 9. The assumption of the defense by the Indemnifying Party will not be construed as an acknowledgment that the Indemnifying Party is liable to indemnify the Indemnified Party with respect to such Claim, nor will it constitute a waiver by the Indemnifying Party of any defenses it may assert against the Indemnified Party's claim for indemnification.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction within Section 9.3 regarding the Indemnified Party's ability to settle claims independently. The original text stipulates that while the Indemnifying Party is actively defending the claim in good faith, the Indemnified Party cannot settle or compromise the claim without the Indemnifying Party's prior written consent. The changed text adds: 'Notwithstanding the above, the Indemnified Party reserves the right to independently settle the claim at any point, regardless of the Indemnifying Party's involvement.' This new sentence directly contradicts the requirement for the Indemnifying Party's consent, creating an irreconcilable inconsistency as to whether the Indemnified Party can settle independently or must obtain consent.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive three (3) year terms thereafter (each a "Renewal Term" and together with the Initial Term, the "Term") until and unless (i) either Party provides the other Party written notice of non-renewal no later than ninety (90) days prior the end of the Initial Term or any Renewal Term or (ii) earlier terminated as provided in this ARTICLE 11.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive three (3) year terms thereafter (each a "Renewal Term" and together with the Initial Term, the "Term") until and unless (i) either Party provides the other Party written notice of non-renewal no later than ninety (90) days prior the end of the Initial Term or any Renewal Term or (ii) earlier terminated as provided in this ARTICLE 11. **However, SIGA reserves the unilateral right to terminate the automatic renewal clause at any time with 30 days written notice, regardless of the current term.**
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence gives SIGA the right to terminate the automatic renewal with 30 days' notice, which directly contradicts the initial clause stating that non-renewal requires 90 days' notice before the end of a term, creating an uncertainty. The original 90 day notice implies a mutual agreement, while the new clause implies that the agreement can be altered at any time, leading to ambiguity in Section 11.1. In-text Contradiction in Section 11.1 in regards to who is in charge of terminating the renewal term of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement becomes effective on the Effective Date and, unless earlier terminated as provided in this ARTICLE 11, shall continue until the five (5) year anniversary of the Effective Date (the "Initial Term"). This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for successive three (3) year terms thereafter (each a "Renewal Term" and together with the Initial Term, the "Term") until and unless (i) either Party provides the other Party written notice of non-renewal no later than ninety (90) days prior the end of the Initial Term or any Renewal Term or (ii) earlier terminated as provided in this ARTICLE 11. **However, SIGA reserves the unilateral right to terminate the automatic renewal clause at any time with 30 days written notice, regardless of the current term.**
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a unilateral right for SIGA to terminate the automatic renewal with 30 days' notice, which directly contradicts the original clause requiring 90 days' notice for non-renewal by either party before the end of a term. This creates a strong inconsistency regarding the conditions for terminating the renewal term, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The contradicted text (which is now also the changed text) now includes two conflicting conditions for ending the agreement creating an in-text contradiction.
